coverage: data export and BTSDA analysis entry points
notes: This manual is primarily a BTSClient software-operation manual, not a tester hardware datasheet.
Use it to answer what the Neware software can configure, export, inspect, or protect.
Do not use it alone to claim a specific channel current range, voltage range, or thermal-control capability for one tester model.

manuals.model: Ivium potentiostat / battery tester cell-cable quick guide
coverage: automatic data saving paths and file types
notes: This quick guide is valuable because the electrode wiring diagrams carry operational meaning.
Use it for generic Ivium cable-to-electrode mapping, first-measurement startup, and saved-file workflow.
Do not use it alone to infer hardware current range, voltage limits, or FRA performance of one exact Ivium model.

coverage: IviumSoft installation and software package location
instrument connection and channel selection by serial number
notes: This guide is best used for software readiness, basic connection, and instrument self-check workflow.
It is useful for maintenance and preflight support, but not as a substitute for a model-specific Ivium hardware specification.

coverage: current-versus-voltage perturbation choice for low-impedance systems
notes: Use this note to explain practical EIS setup theory and why galvanostatic perturbation is often preferred for low-impedance battery systems.
Do not use it alone as a hardware-limit specification or as a full battery-EIS theory source.

manuals.model: Ivium EIS measurement setup application note
coverage: available EIS method families in IviumSoft
AutoCR, Pre ranging, and DualCR behavior
advanced-mode fields including Connect to and Apply wrt OCP
notes: This note is the best current Ivium source for how to configure an EIS measurement in software.
Use it for software workflow and parameter-field interpretation, not as proof of model-specific hardware capability.

manuals.model: Ivium EIS equivalent circuit fitting application note
coverage: element dictionary for R, C, W, Q, L, T, O, and G
notes: Use this note as the Ivium-specific syntax and workflow reference for ECM fitting.
Do not treat the availability of an element in the software as proof that it is physically justified for a given battery dataset.

manuals.model: Ivium EIS worked example application note
coverage: starter EIS settings in a worked example
notes: Use this note as a starter worked example for first-use Ivium EIS questions.
Do not generalize the example settings to every battery without checking cell context and hardware capability.

coverage: current and voltage compliance for the standard configuration
potentiostat, galvanostat, and impedance-analyser operating envelopes
boundaries between low-current characterization and high-current battery testing
notes: This is the first Ivium asset in the repository that can directly support model-specific capability questions such as whether the hardware is suitable for low-current battery EIS.
Interpret the 30 mA, 1 A, and mode-specific limits carefully: the datasheet mixes potentiostat, galvanostat, and impedance-analyser specifications.
Do not assume optional expanded-current configurations or booster modules are part of the standard unit unless separately documented.

coverage: potentiostat, galvanostat, and impedance-analyser operating envelopes
frequency range up to 8 MHz and 2 MHz acquisition rate
safety limit settings for current and potential
notes: This asset captures a more battery-capable Ivium model than CompactStat2.h standard and is useful for moderate-current single-cell testing decisions.

Interpret the 5 A compliance and 10 A range statements carefully: for the standard instrument, the safest planning ceiling is the top-level 5 A compliance unless additional validated hardware documentation says otherwise.
Do not assume optional modules such as the Bipotentiostat or True Linear Scan generator are installed unless separately confirmed.
page_spans_used: user-supplied specification text, screenshots, and product URL

coverage: single-channel high-voltage and high-current battery-testing capability
pulse and DCIR support with 500ms minimum pulse width
parallel-channel note and pulse disable boundary
notes: This is the high-power Neware option in the repository and is suited to single-channel pack or module work rather than high-throughput screening.
Treat the 500 ms minimum pulse width and the pulse-disabled parallel mode as hard constraints when answering method-suitability questions.

coverage: pulse and DCIR support with 500ms minimum pulse width
notes: This is the low-voltage, multi-channel Neware option and is better for throughput and small-cell workflows than for high-power tests.
Treat the 5V ceiling, 6A ceiling, and 30W per-channel limit together when judging suitability.

coverage: pulse workflows with 500ms minimum pulse width
higher-current single-cell suitability compared with 5V6A systems
notes: This unit fills the middle ground between the low-current 5V6A rack and the high-voltage 100V60A single-channel tester.
Treat the 5V ceiling, 30A current range, and 150W per-channel rating as the primary planning limits.

manuals.model: LIT MK 240 / LIT MK 720 battery test chambers
coverage: model scope and variants for LIT MK 240 and LIT MK 720
temperature envelope, ramp rates, and unloaded-chamber technical data
EUCAR hazard-level boundaries for cells, modules, and systems
defined-load limits for EUCAR 4 to 6 operation
gas detection, CO2 fire suppression, pressure relief, and inertization interfaces
MB2 controller workflow and safety-controller behavior
operator responsibilities, SOP expectations, and logbook requirements
notes: This is not just a generic thermal-chamber manual; it is a battery-test-chamber safety and operating manual.
Use it to answer whether the chamber is suitable for battery ageing or thermal testing under specific EUCAR and sample-load assumptions.
Do not treat the chamber as explosion-protected; the manual explicitly excludes EUCAR hazard level 7.

coverage: real-time IR and Coulombic Efficiency metrics mapping
notes: This is an Ultra-High Precision Coulometry (HPC) unit.
Use this for checking precision claims against long-term, low-current degradation tests.

coverage: 100ppm measurement precision across four current ranges
0 to 5V working envelope natively scalable to 6V
high-current modules handling 50A to 300A with massive parallelization up to 1200A
true bipolar circuitry for cross-zero linearity in dynamic pulses
notes: Use this for general purpose, high-power cycling constraints.
Zero-switch delay makes this ideal for confirming execution viability of rapid rest/pulse HPPC waveforms.

coverage: 85% regenerative energy recapture to the facility grid
up to 64 channels per rack spanning up to 1600A fully parallelized
notes: Highly optimized for extreme current draw validation and life-cycling large packs securely and sustainably.
Store the raw PDF in this folder or a child folder and reference it from source_file.
Coverage should capture what the manual can support, for example current limits, sampling constraints, triggers, or safety interlocks.
